title: Dialect Portability, NoSQL Tradeoffs, Distribution, and Production Defense · Stakeholder Data Mission
summary: A privacy-conscious software organization. The organization needs a portable data system plan with safe access, least privilege, recovery, observability, and justified storage choices. Planned artifact: Deliver Production Data System Defense with acceptance evidence, rubric defense, reflection, and a changed-constraint extension. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Bind untrusted values through prepared statements while allow-listing unavoidable identifiers or directions.
Minimize collected fields, purpose, access, retention, export, and re-identification risk across the lifecycle.
Separate standard relational intent from SQLite and PostgreSQL syntax, typing, NULL, date, and feature behavior.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.sql:
-- sql-basics cumulative relational data portfolio.
-- Each increment declares grain, predicts cardinality or changed rows, runs in fresh browser SQLite,
-- reconciles results, tests changed data, and records PostgreSQL or standard-SQL boundaries.

SELECT sqlite_version() AS browser_sqlite_version;

content.code:
-- Evidence: sql-capstone-defense-1nrfi7
-- grain: declare the exact row or mutation grain before running
EXPLAIN QUERY PLAN
SELECT team_id, COUNT(*) AS backlog
FROM tickets WHERE status <> 'resolved'
GROUP BY team_id ORDER BY team_id;
-- observation: reconcile the returned rows, changed count, error, or plan with the prediction
